title: Induction, Decision and Game Theory
description: Formal methods relevant to probabilistic and inductive reasoning. Decision theory, game theory, axiomatic probability theory and its interpretations, belief dynamics, simulation and modelling.

title: Modal Logic
description: Logic of the modal operators 'it is necessary that' and 'it is possible that'. Possible-world semantics and a method of derivation for this logic.

title: Ethics for the Sciences
description: Philosophical exploration of ethical issues in the non-medical sciences, including topics such as intellectual integrity, responsible conduct of research, and protection of human subjects.

title: Introduction to History and Philosophy of Science
description: An examination of the historical, conceptual and methodological conditions of scientific knowledge through detailed consideration of important episodes in the history of science.

title: Philosophy of Science
description: Issues common to all sciences. Philosophical questions including the character of scientific laws, theories and revolutions, the nature of scientific confirmation, causality, explanation and prediction.

title: Introduction to Scientific Reasoning
description: Historical and logical analysis of various types of scientific hypotheses and the data that support or undermine them.

title: Symbolic Logic
description: Sentential and predicate logic. Translation from natural language; truth tables and interpretations; systems of natural deduction up to relational predicate logic with identity.
